replication_study.hypothesis: There is a statistically-significant Level × Product-Type interaction on participants’ attitude-function difference scores (Benefit – Symbolism). Specifically, for utilitarian product categories the mean difference score will become relatively smaller (more symbolic) when the product is evaluated at the brand level than at the category (unbranded) level, whereas for symbolic product categories the mean difference score will become relatively larger (more utilitarian) at the brand level than at the category level.

data_plan.wave_or_subset: Single wave; we retain only complete cases that pass the two attention checks and did not previously participate (screenQ == 2)
data_plan.sample_size: Final N will be determined by the exclusion rules but starts from 637 responses (LeBoeuf_replication_data.csv, 637 × 111; get_dataset_shape).
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Individual participant (ID) with a repeated-measures factor for ProductType (Utilitarian vs Symbolic)
data_plan.access_details: The raw .csv (LeBoeuf_replication_data.csv) and the item list (ItemsList_Final.csv) are bundled in /app/data under data/original/23/input/replication_data.

qualification.explanation: The dataset reproduces the original experimental design (2 × 2 mixed), uses the identical eight focal products and the same benefit/symbolism rating scales, but collects responses from a new, independent online sample. This allows a direct replication test of the focal interaction while providing an independent data realization.
qualification.similarity_to_original:
• Contains the same eight products split into utilitarian vs symbolic categories (ItemsList_Final.csv; cf. original stimuli described in post_registration.json → method → steps #1).
• Retains the Level manipulation (Cond=1/2=Category, 3/4=Brand) exactly matching the original between-subjects factor (coded in LeBoeuf_replication_data.csv; variable ‘Cond’; see loaded column list).
• Uses identical 7-point benefit and symbolism scales (column prefixes “Util” and “Symbol” identical to coding outlined in the original paper – initial_details.txt).
qualification.deviation_from_original:
• Original participants were in-lab undergraduates (post_registration.json → data → source). The replication sample is an online convenience panel collected in 2020 (LeBoeuf_replication_data.csv → StartDate 11/3/20), offering a demographically broader but different population.
• Original had N≈206 for Study 1b; replication has up to N≈600 after exclusions, providing higher power.
• Brand-name sets A & B were randomised into Cond 3 and 4, whereas the original Study 1 only compared one brand set; thus we combine Cond 3 and 4 to recreate the single Brand level as done in the provided R code.

data_plan.notes: The item list is supplied as .csv not .xlsx, so the analysis script must read ItemsList_Final.csv instead of ItemsList_Final.xlsx. All file paths must reference /app/data.

steps: Apply the preregistered exclusion rules: Status==0, screenQ non-missing & ==2, Attention1==7, Attention2==1.
Drop administrative columns, impose participant ID, remove familiarity and bipolar items as in original script.
Create data frames for Category (Cond 1 & 2) and Brand (Cond 3 & 4) conditions; convert from wide to long so each row = participant × item.
Compute ScoreDiff = Benefits – Symbols for every item response.
Aggregate to participant × ProductType × Level (Category vs Brand) means.
Run a 2(Level: Category vs Brand, between) × 2(ProductType: Utilitarian vs Symbolic, within) mixed ANOVA: aov(ScoreDiff ~ Condition*ProductType + Error(ID/ProductType)).
Extract the F-statistic, p-value, partial η² for the interaction, and plot condition means with SE bars.
planned_method.independent_variables: Condition/Level (Category vs Brand), ProductType (Utilitarian vs Symbolic)
planned_method.control_variables: Participant random effect (ID), counterbalancing inherent in within-subject design
planned_method.tools_software: R (>=4.2) with tidyverse, readr, readxl, emmeans, sjstats, reshape2, ggpubr, etc.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Partial η² for the Level × ProductType interaction
planned_estimation_and_test.test: F-test from mixed ANOVA with df(1, N–#groups)

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion via preregistered filters; no imputation.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Not applicable – single focal interaction; descriptive post-hoc contrasts reported without multiplicity correction (as in original).
planned_method.inference_criteria: Reject H0 if p < .05 (two-tailed) for the interaction; direction must match theoretical pattern.

files.replication_data/LeBoeuf_JournMarketRes_: Main analysis script adapted for replication (paths set to /app/data).

codebase.notes: All scripts are written in R and assume working directory '/app'. Output tables/figures will be saved to /app/data/results/.

analysis.instructions: 1) docker run with mounted ./data. 2) Rscript "replication_data/LeBoeuf_JournMarketRes_2010_EKBZ_Yoon_Direct Replication_618K_Final.R". 3) Inspect console output and /app/data/results/interaction_results.csv and plot.png.
analysis.comparison_metrics: Compare the replication F(1, df) and p-value for the Level × ProductType interaction to the original F(1, 204)=7.70, p=.006 (initial_details.txt). Also compare the direction of cell means.
